rationale: Lighting consoles demand a deep dark mode to prevent light bleed in theater environments, high legibility, and unmistakable interaction states. Archetype 4's brutalist function-first approach ensures operators hit the right cues instantly without visual clutter.

sections.cue_grid: col-span-1 lg:col-span-8 grid grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-3

surfaces_and_micro_themes.header: Crystal Glass variant: bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-white/10 z-50 sticky top-0
surfaces_and_micro_themes.cards: Grid Borders look: flat surface (#121212), 1px solid border-white/10, no shadow. Active elements get a brighter border.
surfaces_and_micro_themes.buttons: Tactile switch aesthetic. Base state is dark grey with a 1px border. Active state has full color background (Green/Red) and glowing drop shadow.
surfaces_and_micro_themes.log_panel: Retro-Futurism Terminal Look: Deep black background, border-white/10, tiny monospace green text, scanline texture if possible.

components_instructions.general: Use pure HTML/Tailwind for the grid layout. Use Shadcn components for dialogs, forms, and inputs.
components_instructions.cue_buttons: Large touch targets (h-24), display cue number prominently. State: Idle (border-white/10 text-white/70), Active/Hover (border-green-500 text-green-400 bg-green-500/10).
components_instructions.stop_all_button: Massive target (h-16), bright red background hover:bg-red-600 text-white font-bold.
components_instructions.settings_modal: Shadcn Dialog. Form includes input for ngrok URL and port.
components_instructions.connection_indicator: Small pulsing dot next to header title. Green if connected, Red if offline.
components_instructions.command_logs: Fixed height scrollable area (h-64 flex-grow), auto-scroll to bottom, retro terminal style.

motion_and_interaction.hover_states: Hard crisp changes. Buttons light up their borders or backgrounds instantly. No slow fades (this is hardware control).
motion_and_interaction.active_states: Slight scale down (scale-95) to simulate a physical hardware button press.
motion_and_interaction.animations: Keep minimal. A pulsing dot for connection status. Smooth slide-in for the settings dialog.

media_and_assets.icons: lucide-react (Settings, Activity, Square, Power, AlertTriangle, Terminal)

image_urls.description: Wavy metallic surface with small dots. Use as a very low opacity (5%) fixed background to give the dashboard a tactile hardware feel.
image_urls.usage: Global background texture overlaid behind the main app container.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install required fonts: Chivo, IBM Plex Sans, JetBrains Mono via Google Fonts.
Setup Tailwind config with the specified color palette and extend theme.
Ensure all buttons, inputs, and toggles have `data-testid` attributes (e.g., `data-testid="cue-btn-1"`, `data-testid="stop-all-btn"`).
Build a robust layout: A sticky top header (branding, connection status, settings trigger). Main content area split into Cue Grid (left) and Master Controls + Logs (right).
Implement the Command Log panel as an auto-scrolling `div` with a retro terminal look.
Use Shadcn Dialog for the Settings page, capturing 'host' and 'port' state. Do not use generic browser prompts.
Respect the 'Control Room Grid' spacing rule: use tight gaps (`gap-3` or `gap-4`) for the cue buttons so they look like a physical MIDI controller bank.
The app has .js files. DO NOT USE TypeScript (.tsx/ts). Provide guidelines explicitly for React Javascript.
